name: Palencia Cultural Route
name_original: City tour por Palencia — circular por el centro histórico y por la ribera del río Carrión
description: Explore Palencia's historic center and the tranquil Carrión River banks on this easy 3.8-mile (6.1 km) circular hiking route.
region_intro: Hiking around Palencia offers diverse landscapes, from the high peaks and deep valleys of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park to the tranquil banks of the Canal de Castilla and the expansive Castilian plains. The region features a variety of terrain, including ancient forests, meandering rivers, and unique geological formations. These natural features provide a wide range of hiking experiences across the province. Best hiking trails around Palencia The most popular hiking route is La Casa Grande de Monte el Viejo — circular route, an easy 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route explores the accessible natural area close to the city. Another top favourite among local hikers is Canal de Castilla between Palencia and Villamuriel de Cerrato — circular along the banks of the río Carrión, a moderate 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This trail follows the historic Canal de Castilla, offering flat terrain along its tranquil banks. Local hikers also love the Palencia Cultural Route, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through Palencia's historic center and along the Carrión River banks, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Hiking around Palencia is defined by majestic mountains, verdant valleys, and the historic Canal de Castilla. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding excursions. The routes in Palencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Palencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Palencia?
A: Palencia offers nearly 100 hiking routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls along the Canal de Castilla to more challenging mountain excursions in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Palencia?
A: Palencia's landscapes are incredibly diverse. You can explore the majestic mountains and deep valleys of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, the tranquil banks of the historic Canal de Castilla, unique geological formations in the Las Tuerces Natural Area, and the expansive Castilian plains which transform with the seasons.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes near Palencia city?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365738323/la-casa-grande-de-monte-el-viejo-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casa Grande de Monte el Viejo — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile trail in an accessible natural area close to the city. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062356483/palencia-cultural-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia Cultural Route&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the city's historic center and the Carrión River banks.

Q: Where can I find circular hiking routes in Palencia?
A: Many routes in Palencia are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365738323/la-casa-grande-de-monte-el-viejo-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casa Grande de Monte el Viejo — circular route&lt;/a&gt; near the city, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062370679/canal-de-castilla-between-palencia-and-villamuriel-de-cerrato-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-rio-carrion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla between Palencia and Villamuriel de Cerrato — circular along the banks of the río Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the historic waterway.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural features to see on hikes in Palencia?
A: Absolutely. In the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, you can discover impressive waterfalls like Covalagua and Mazobre. The park also features the Tejeda de Tosande, home to one of Europe's oldest yew groves, and the Cueva de los Franceses with its stunning geological formations. The Las Tuerces Natural Area offers a unique 'lunar landscape' with curious karst formations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Palencia?
A: Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in Palencia, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Spring brings wildflowers, while autumn showcases rich forest colors. Summer can be warm, especially in the plains, but the higher altitudes of Montaña Palentina offer cooler conditions. Winter hiking is possible, particularly in the mountains, but requires appropriate gear for snow and colder weather.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Palencia?
A: Many trails in Palencia are dog-friendly, especially those in natural parks and along the Canal de Castilla. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Palencia?
A: The hiking routes in Palencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse natural landscapes, from the dramatic mountains to the peaceful Canal de Castilla, and the variety of trails suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Palencia?
A: Yes, the Montaña Palentina Natural Park offers more demanding excursions. Peaks like Pico Curavacas and Pico Espigüete provide challenging climbs and offer unparalleled panoramic views for experienced hikers. These routes often involve significant elevation gain and rugged terrain.

Q: Are there options for public transport to reach hiking trails in Palencia province?
A: While some trails closer to Palencia city, like those in Monte El Viejo or along the Canal de Castilla, might be accessible by local transport, reaching trails deeper within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park often requires a car. Public transport options to the more remote trailheads are limited, so planning ahead is essential.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Palencia?
A: The Montaña Palentina Natural Park is a haven for wildlife. Hikers might spot species such as the Cantabrian brown bear, Iberian wolf, Cantabrian capercaillie, golden eagle, and griffon vulture. The park's diverse flora also includes ancient forests of beech, oak, and juniper.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Palencia is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062356483/palencia-cultural-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia Cultural Route&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the city's historic center. The Canal de Castilla, an 18th-century engineering marvel, offers routes along its banks where you can explore its history and the towns along its path. Sections of the Camino Francés pilgrimage route also pass through Palencia, offering cultural immersion.
tips:
The work is located in the Plaza Mayor at the intersection of Calle Mayor with Calle Los Soldados and was made by the sculptor Luis Alonso in 2017 on the occasion of the fiftieth anniversary of the sculptor's death.
The 2.5-meter-long sculpture, made of bronze, depicts the distinguished artist from Palencia working on his masterpiece and unequivocal symbol of Palencia, the Cristo del Otero.

The City Hall of Palencia is located in the Plaza Mayor. The square is surrounded by classical buildings.

The Plaza Mayor in Palencia has had various functions throughout its history. Bullfights took place there, it was a place of leisure and a place of justice. Until the construction of the Mercado de Abastos in 1898, the market was also held there.

The work is located in the Paseo del Salón de Isabel II and was carried out in 2006 by the Palencia sculptor Sergio García Díez.

The sculpture is made up of a sculptural group made of bronze and made up of two statues in which two old people can be seen, specifically a woman and a man who appear in a resting position seated on the bench. The man also leans on his cachaba and the woman rests her hands on her legs, seeming to engage in a pleasant conversation.


The statue was inaugurated on January 10, 2006 by the Palencia City Council, as a tribute to the elderly in Palencia.

A bridge of Roman origin, renovated in the 15th century, which was an important enclave in the development of road and commercial networks from the Vaccean period to the beginning of the 20th century. There are no remains of its Roman origin, but its layout from medieval times. Currently, pilgrimages and medieval markets are held in its surroundings.

On a rectangular granite base, there are two characters made of bronze: a teacher, who appears seated on a pedestal with an open book in his hands, in the attitude of reading it to a girl, who is sitting on the I usually listen carefully.

Made by the sculptor, Rafael Cordero, a teacher by profession.
The Plaza de la Inmaculada, in the Castilian-Leonese city of Palencia and where the Cathedral of San Antolín is located

The Cathedral of Palencia is dedicated to San Antolín, patron saint of the city of Palencia. Popularly known as 'the unknown beauty', it was declared a Historic-Artistic Monument in 1929. The current Palencia Cathedral, in Gothic style, stands on a previous Romanesque construction, of which only a few remains remain.

The work is located in the Plaza de San Pablo, it was made by the Madrid sculptor Oscar Alvariño and inaugurated on March 14, 2008.

This monument, also known as "The call of the brother brothers and the touch of the Tararú", and made of bronze, is made up of two figures that represent the call of brothers and the touch of the "Tararú". The characters measure 2.40 meters and are placed on a stone base.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the City tour of Palencia — circular through the historic center and along the banks of the Carrión river?
faqs.answer: This easy circular route typically takes approximately 1 hour and 34 minutes to complete, making it a pleasant stroll through Palencia's urban and natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is classified as easy and is suitable for all fitness levels, including families. Its pedestrian-friendly nature makes it an enjoyable experience for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this city tour?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, combining paved streets and squares in the historic center with tranquil riverside paths along the Carrión River. You'll walk through parks, groves, and gardens, offering a mix of urban and natural surfaces.

faqs.question: What are some of the main sights I will see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a rich blend of historical and natural landmarks. Highlights include the Plaza Mayor of Palencia, the impressive Cathedral of San Antolín of Palencia, and the historic Puente de Puentecillas. The route also passes by the Isabel II Park and the Church of San Pablo of Palencia.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails, including parts of the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Sur), Vía Verde del Tren Burra, and various sections of the Pasaje Gil de Fuentes and Pasaje Habana.

faqs.question: Is the City tour of Palencia dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific rules for dogs in Palencia's historic center and parks may apply. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in busy urban areas and near wildlife along the riverbanks.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit Palencia for this walking tour?
faqs.answer: Palencia offers a pleasant experience year-round. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures ideal for walking. Summer can be warm, but the riverside sections offer shade. Winter is generally cooler but can still be enjoyable for a city stroll.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to walk this circular route through Palencia's historic center and along the Carrión riverbanks. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting Palencia for this city tour?
faqs.answer: As this is an urban route, you can find various public parking options in and around Palencia's city center. Look for municipal parking garages or street parking, particularly on the outskirts of the most central pedestrian zones, to begin your tour easily.

faqs.question: What kind of natural features or wildlife might I encounter along the Carrión river?
faqs.answer: Along the Carrión River, you'll experience a more natural environment with poplars, ivy, and reeds marking its course. The parks and gardens, like Dos Aguas Park and Sotillo de los Canónigos, offer a chance to observe local flora and fauna within an urban setting, including various bird species.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through Palencia's historic center, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops, especially around the lively Plaza Mayor of Palencia and along the pedestrian Calle Mayor, perfect for a break or a meal.
